source_note: Thirty analysis years gives roughly 7-10 candidate 3-5-year cycles after burn-in.
Long burn-in reduces dependence on arbitrary initial infections and immunity.
Working default retained for scenario analysis; country-level incidence calibration remains a next step against the 2023 surveillance window.
Assumption for subclinical/mild transmission under aP-like protection; retained in sensitivity analysis.
Country profiles infer 3-5-year periodicity from annual surveillance peaks when visible; otherwise use 4 years.
Weak phase-locking term; main interpretation remains susceptible depletion/replenishment plus seasonal forcing.
Uses 8 days within CDC's common 7-10 day incubation range.
Uses 21 days, aligned with infectiousness through roughly the third week after paroxysm onset.
Shorter infectious duration for mild/asymptomatic infections; sensitivity range should be retained.
Uses 9 years within CDC's 4-20 year observational range; longer natural-immunity values remain sensitivity cases because the current ODE has no explicit immune-boosting state.
Uses 5 years to approximate rapid aP waning; meta-analysis shows marked decline by 3-7 years.

description: Daily transition from symptomatic infection to treatment
source_note: Sensitive-strain treatment and PEP assumptions reflect early macrolide benefit; resistant effect is a scenario assumption.

description: Dynamic PEP coverage ceiling among close contacts
source_note: Approximation of household/close-contact PEP reach in an age-structured model without explicit households.
